description: Set up autoresearch harness and recorded baseline render benchmark

Tried ASCII-specific to_ascii_uppercase path in upper filter for lowercase ASCII strings
Add html-escape precheck and direct write_str fast path for strings without escapable chars
Specialized HTML formatter for primitive ValueRepr variants to bypass Value Display dispatch
Attempted to force-inline write_with_html_escaping in utils hot path
Tried simpler any(matches!) implementation for needs_html_escaping byte scan

Second control run; observed benchmark noise drifting higher on current machine load
Bypass formatter callback for default formatter using formatter_is_default fast path
Tried force-inlining Environment::format after adding default formatter fast path
Rewrote undefined-check match in Environment::format as early-return if branch
Forced inline on get_or_lookup_local cache helper in VM
Added aggressive inlining hints to VM stack push/pop and call-arg helpers
Specialize VM Emit for default formatter to bypass Environment::format call and branch directly to write_escaped
Tested Value handle registry switch from BTreeMap to HashMap for Value::serialize roundtrip path
Tried larger initial VM stack capacity (32) to reduce Value Vec reallocations
Checked existing unit assertion confirming Value size is 24 bytes on 64-bit targets
Tried unsafe unchecked VM stack pop to reduce Option/unwrap overhead on Value moves
Refactored internal Value roundtrip handle registry from keyed map to stack-based pop path in serializer
Switched VM frame locals from BTreeMap to HashMap to test faster local lookup/store
Hoisted default formatter and strict undefined checks out of VM loop for Emit instruction
Tried manual ASCII lowercase byte scan in upper filter no-op fast path
Repeat measurement for manual ASCII lowercase scan in upper fast path
Added Object::get_value_by_str path and specialized map lookups to avoid temporary Value key allocations (run 1)
Add Object::get_value_by_str fast path and optimize Value-keyed map string lookups for small maps
Tried manual loop implementation for small Value-map string lookups instead of iterator find_map
Tuned Value-map get_value_by_str linear-scan threshold from <=8 to <=16
Tuned Value-map get_value_by_str linear-scan threshold from <=8 to <=4
Control run on current best code to observe machine noise after Value lookup optimization
Forced inline on Value::get_attr and get_attr_fast after get_value_by_str refactor
Added get_value_by_str overrides for MergeDict and Namespace objects to avoid temporary Value keys
Tried splitting Option arg-count handling at VM call sites to favor fixed-arity paths
Use string-keyed BTreeMap in hidden context! builder to avoid Value-key conversion overhead

Tried static-str context map keys with direct Object impl for BTreeMap<&'static str, _> to eliminate key allocations
Prototype context! ContextMap object failed to compile due Debug impl using wrong iterator item type
Tested custom Vec-backed ContextMap object for context! macro to avoid tree map overhead
Match ValueRepr::String/SmallStr directly in small Value-map string lookup fast path
Tried manual loop instead of find_map closure for small Value-map string-key lookup
Tried thread-local Arc<str> key interning in context! builder to avoid repeated Arc allocations
Add Loop::get_value_by_str fast path for loop attribute access to skip temporary Value key creation
Tried VM-level special case for loop.index in GetAttr to bypass generic object attribute lookup
Tried restructuring Loop::get_value_by_str to defer len computation to specific match arms
Forced inline on Loop get_value/get_value_by_str after loop attribute fast path
Forced inline(always) for Output write methods and fmt::Write impl methods
Control run on current best commit to monitor benchmark variance after recent experiments
Tried early-return fast path for loop.index before full Loop attribute match
Cached loop object Value in LoopState to avoid rebuilding Value::from_dyn_object on loop lookups
Retried ASCII to_ascii_uppercase path for upper filter after recent VM/object optimizations

Use Vec-backed Locals with linear lookup/update instead of BTreeMap for small VM scope frames
Tried manual for-loop implementations for Locals insert/get instead of iterator find/find_map
Preallocate Vec-backed Locals with capacity 4 to reduce scope-local insertion reallocations
Tune initial Locals vector capacity from 4 to 8 for lower reallocation overhead
Run interrupted by late-night continuation guard before benchmark execution
Further tune initial Locals vector capacity from 8 to 16
Tune initial Locals vector capacity from 16 to 32 for lower allocation churn in deep loop scopes
Tried increasing Locals initial vector capacity to 64
Expanded harness to also emit parse/compile secondary metrics; render metric slightly higher on first run
Expand autoresearch harness with parse/compile secondary metrics while preserving render as primary
Tried Locals initial capacity 24 instead of 32 under expanded multi-metric harness
Tried Locals initial capacity 40 instead of 32
Tried reverse-iteration strategy for Locals get/insert to favor recently touched variables
Added aggressive inline(always) hints to Vec-backed Locals helper methods
Retested larger VM evaluation stack capacity (32) with current optimized locals design
Replaced Value handle BTreeMap with Vec<Option<Value>> indexed storage for serde roundtrip path
Tried last-entry fast path in Locals::insert for repeated writes to same variable
Added first-entry shortcut in Locals::get before linear scan
Tried pointer-equality fast path for Vec-backed Locals key matching before string equality
Tried Locals last-lookup index cache via Cell to speed repeated variable access

Tried lowering Value-map string-lookup linear-scan threshold from <=12 to <=10

Tried reverse-order scan only in Locals::insert to favor recent variable rewrites
Tried reverse-order scan in Locals::get to favor most recently inserted locals
Forced inline(always) on Stack::get_call_args
Tried macro-level fast path to bypass Value::from_serialize for nested context! expressions
Tried split-key/value Vec representation for Locals to reduce value loads during key scans

Tried manual for-loop implementation for Locals::get instead of iterator find_map

Retested Locals initial capacity 24 under current best (stack-24, frame-stack-40) baseline
Tried increasing Value-map string lookup linear-scan threshold from <=12 to <=14
Tried lowering Value-map string lookup linear-scan threshold from <=12 to <=11
Baseline: Value size is 24 bytes on 64-bit target (value::tests::test_sizes)

name: Recover render performance after Value size reduction

description: Add Seq ValueRepr variant for serialized/iter-collected lists to avoid Arc<DynObject> wrapper overhead in render hot path
Add StrMap ValueRepr variant for context! maps and direct map attr/item paths to reduce Arc<DynObject> overhead while keeping 16-byte Value
Cache LoopState object Value and reuse StrMap/Seq variants to avoid repeated Arc<DynObject> allocations; recovers most render regression while keeping 16-byte Value
Fix StrMap semantic regression with minimal overhead: make dict() emit StrMap only when cloning an existing map with string keys, preserving kwargs behavior and snapshot order
Restore compatibility for Seq/StrMap in ArgType and mixed-map equality/ordering while retaining performance; tests pass and render slightly improves
Post-compaction control run on current StrMap/Seq-compatible 16-byte Value branch; observed temporary drift upward (~32.1us) likely machine noise
Speed up VM locals lookup/update by replacing iterator-closure scans with manual loops and pointer-equality fast checks for interned variable names
Confirmation run after locals pointer-equality patch (no code changes); measured ~30.2us, indicating positive noise but experiment already captured
Tried thread-local Arc<str> interning for context! keys to avoid per-render key allocations; hash/refcell overhead regressed render throughput
Tried precomputing key=="loop" once in Context::load to skip repeated loop-var checks; no gain and regressed
Tried manual for-loop implementation for Context::current_loop to avoid iterator adapter overhead; render regressed significantly
Add primitive fast paths in ArgType<Cow<str>> (u64/i64/bool) to avoid generic Value Display conversion overhead on hot filter calls (notably item|upper)
Control rerun on same primitive string-conversion fast path showed substantial noise drift upward (~30.7us); keep prior improved code but treat single-run variance cautiously
Tried small static cache for common integer->string conversions in ArgType<Cow<str>> plus bool static borrow; slightly worse than prior primitive fast-path approach
Tried callable cache in VM with BoxedFunction fast-path dispatch for filters/tests to bypass Value::call; extra classification/indirection regressed heavily
Added specialized builtin upper_filter(Value)->Value and registered it as default upper, bypassing generic Cow<String> arg marshalling and using allocation-free integer/boolean uppercase paths
Fixed strict/semi-strict undefined semantics in specialized upper_filter by adding state-based undefined checks; correctness restored but performance regressed notably
Retried upper specialization with semantics-preserving Cow->Value wrapper (upper_to_value) to cut no-op string allocations; still slower than ArgType primitive fast-path baseline

Reverted specialized upper_filter fast path from previous run because it violated strict/semi-strict undefined semantics; restored correct built-in upper behavior while retaining major ArgType speedups
Tried single-pass ASCII/lowercase detection in upper filter to replace is_ascii()+any double scan; regressed on workload
Tried bool-to-string static borrow in ArgType<Cow<str>> to avoid tiny allocation; no benefit on this workload
Reintroduced specialized upper_filter with strict-undefined correctness via custom CheckedValue ArgType, plus allocation-free u64/i64/bool uppercase paths
Control rerun on CheckedValue-based upper_filter to gauge variance; still around 27us and confirms large improvement over prior valid baseline
Optimize upper_filter hot path by deferring kwargs-type check to Object branch only, avoiding per-call is_kwargs overhead for primitive/string inputs
Further optimize upper_filter by switching CheckedValue wrapper to direct (&State, &Value) args: avoid Value cloning while preserving strict undefined checks and object-only kwargs guard
Control rerun on (&State,&Value)-based upper_filter optimization confirms stable ~25us performance band
Bypass generic Value::from_function argument marshalling for upper by registering a dedicated UpperFilterObject with direct call(args) dispatch and optimized coercion path
Control rerun on dedicated UpperFilterObject path; confirms another very strong sample (~24.2us) with current implementation
Add VM ApplyFilter fast path for built-in upper: when cached filter is UpperFilterObject, bypass generic Value::call and dispatch directly to call_upper_filter_fast
Tried caching UpperFilterObject downcast classification per local_id in VM ApplyFilter to avoid repeated checks; added branching/state overhead regressed
Tried extra VM upper fast path specialization for single-arg calls (call_upper_filter_one) to skip args slice matching; added branches regressed
Add cached small-u64 Value table for upper filter coercion (0..1023) and reuse for non-negative i64 path, avoiding repeated decimal conversion in hot numeric upper calls
Control rerun after cached small-u64 upper coercion; result remains in ~23.5us range and confirms large win
Tried arg-count-aware one-arg upper VM dispatch (call_upper_filter_one) to skip slice arity match on fixed-arity instructions; no improvement
Tune upper numeric Value cache size from 1024 to 256 entries (covers common small integers with better locality and less initialization overhead)
Control rerun for 256-entry upper numeric cache confirms stable performance around 23.35us
Tried shrinking upper numeric cache to 128 entries; misses for common mid-range integers outweighed locality gains
Tried expanding upper numeric cache to 512 entries; no measurable win versus 256-entry cache
Tried simplifying cached_u64_value to return Value directly (removing Option/unwrap_or path); no measurable gain
Add small-integer HTML output fast path in write_with_html_escaping for U64/I64/Bool using cached string formatting, bypassing generic Value display on hot numeric emits
Control rerun on numeric HTML output fast path confirms stable ~21.7us band
Tried shrinking small-integer HTML output cache from 256 to 128; misses on mid-range loop indices regressed render
Tried expanding small-integer HTML output cache from 256 to 512; no measurable improvement
Baseline run on multi-workload autoresearch harness (all_elements + string_heavy + macro_heavy) after reinitializing target
Optimize context! construction by storing static keys as &'static str maps and adding direct Object map support, avoiding per-key Arc<str> allocations across all render workloads
Control rerun on unchanged static-key context optimization measured lower render_ns (~73.26us); treated as favorable variance until confirmed by additional code-backed gains
Attempted small-map linear-scan fast path for string-keyed map get_value_by_str; failed to compile due ambiguous AsRef type inference in generic map-key macro
Tested small-map linear-scan path for string-keyed BTreeMap attribute lookup (len<=8) to avoid tree lookup overhead; regressed multi-workload render time
Special-case nested context! values in __context_pair to bypass Value::from_serialize roundtrip for context! subexpressions, reducing nested context conversion overhead
Control rerun on nested-context serialization bypass showed higher sample (~74.8us), indicating notable run-to-run noise; keep prior improvement but continue validating
Optimize Value serialization handle registry with a single-slot fast path plus BTreeMap overflow to reduce insert/remove overhead for common immediate Value roundtrips in context construction
Control rerun after single-slot Value handle registry optimization stayed in the same ~73.1us band, confirming improvement but no further gain without code changes
Tried BasicStore single-template fast path in Environment::get to bypass map lookup when only one template is loaded; primary render gain was tiny/noisy and not robust
Preallocated Output capture stack with capacity 4 to reduce capture push reallocations; observed only tiny/noisy primary change on multi-workload benchmark
Replace closure-based internal-serialization OnDrop guard with lightweight dedicated Drop guard to reduce Value::from_serialize guard overhead on hot context construction paths
Remove now-unused OnDrop utility after switching to dedicated serialization guard, reducing dead code and preserving optimized guard path
Control rerun on current optimized branch remained in ~71.3-71.7us range, confirming materially improved performance but no additional gain without code changes

Streamline Value roundtrip signaling by switching handle transport from tuple-struct marker to direct newtype-struct handling in ValueSerializer, removing per-field tuple machinery on hot from_serialize paths
Control rerun on newtype-based Value handle signaling remained in ~71.1-71.3us range, confirming stable improvement without additional code changes
Tried single-slot handle id shortcut (fixed handle 0 fast path) to avoid LAST_VALUE_HANDLE increments for common Value roundtrips; unexpectedly regressed render throughput
Add context! literal fast path in __context_pair to use Value::from directly for literals, bypassing serde-based from_serialize conversion overhead on common literal fields
Control rerun on literal fast path landed in the same improved ~70.8-71.0us band, indicating stable gains with normal measurement variance
Tried context! vector-of-context! specialization to build Value sequences directly from nested context! entries; added macro complexity and regressed overall render throughput
Tried Context::load fast path for frames with empty locals/no loop state to skip map/loop checks; additional branch and is_empty checks regressed multi-workload render
Retested a narrower context! vec-of-context! specialization (brace form only); result was effectively within noise relative to current best and not a robust improvement
Control run on current kept optimizations (no code changes) to re-establish variance band; render remained around 70.8us
Tried one-entry thread-local Arc<str> cache for non-small Value::from(&str) conversions to reuse repeated literal allocations; cache lookup overhead outweighed savings and regressed render
Resume-session control run on current optimized baseline to re-establish variance before new experiments
Tried increasing compile-time buffer_size_hint multiplier from 2x to 3x template raw bytes to reduce render-time String growth reallocations; initial sample improved slightly, pending confirmation
Confirmation run for buffer_size_hint 3x multiplier regressed versus current baseline; treating prior improvement as noise and reverting this tuning
Tried context! fast path for vec![literal, ...] to build Value sequences directly and bypass serde conversion for literal lists; regressed total render time
Tried dedicated HandleSerializer for VALUE_HANDLE_MARKER newtype path to avoid transform(value).as_usize handle extraction; added serializer machinery regressed render throughput
Tried avoiding redundant INTERNAL_SERIALIZATION set(true) when already true in mark_internal_serialization; unexpectedly regressed render throughput
Tried String->Value conversion via Arc::from(val.into_boxed_str()) for large strings to reuse/move string storage path; regressed render workloads
Tried forcing inline(always) on Value::from_serialize to reduce call overhead across context construction; no improvement on multi-workload render benchmark
Refactored context! macro into split no-merge and merge-specific arms to remove empty merge-array branch in common path; unexpectedly caused major render regression
Tried forcing inline(always) on serializing_for_value thread-local check; did not improve and measured substantial regression
Tried reducing buffer_size_hint to 1x raw template bytes (next power of two) to cut initial String allocation size; render regressed versus current 2x heuristic
Tried BasicStore small-map (<=2 templates) linear-scan lookup in Environment::get to reduce BTreeMap lookup overhead for tiny template sets; regressed render
Added positional-only fast path in Macro::call to skip kwargs BTreeSet bookkeeping when no kwargs are present; minor macro_heavy gain was outweighed by regressions elsewhere
Tried replacing Value handle registry overflow storage from BTreeMap to small Vec<(u32, Value)> with linear remove/swap_remove for tiny expected cardinality; slightly regressed render
